change_summary: Audited near-target giveback bad-trade lifecycle family across three fixed OHLCV windows without changing production strategy code.
hypothesis: Accepted-stack bad trades may contain a reproducible near-target giveback family where positions approached their target or built meaningful MFE before reversing into poor exits.
notes: Artifact: docs/experiments/artifacts/exp_20260426_045_near_target_giveback_audit.json. Found 9 bad giveback trades with -1841.18 PnL, but 29 winner-collateral trades with +69710.58 PnL; broad target-progress protection is high collateral risk. Recommended next test is not production code: only consider a much narrower Technology trend giveback partial-lock shadow replay if future evidence warrants.

locked_variables: near-target giveback failure taxonomy for accepted-stack bad trades
parameters.single_causal_variable: near-target giveback failure taxonomy for accepted-stack bad trades
